response.justification: Bogdan is a capable full-stack generalist with solid distributed systems exposure and Staff-level titles, but the resume shows no meaningful experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, telemetry infrastructure, or growth engineering — which are the core competencies for this Principal-level role. The candidate's work at Dunnhumby with high-throughput micro-services is relevant adjacent experience, but there is no evidence of statistical analysis, experiment design, metrics pipelines, or cross-team experimentation tooling. The gap between current scope and what a Principal IC6 growth engineer is expected to own is substantial.
strengths: Demonstrated distributed systems exposure handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby, showing familiarity with latency-sensitive, high-throughput backend work
Broad polyglot background (C, C++, TypeScript, Java, D, Node.js) matching several required language preferences in the JD
Staff-level individual contributor roles with mentorship, devops ownership, and cross-discipline contributions (mobile, embedded, web), indicating some IC seniority trajectory
concerns: Zero experimentation platform experience: no A/B testing, feature flagging, experiment assignment, bias detection, or rollout-risk management mentioned anywhere in the resume
No telemetry, metrics, or observability infrastructure work; the data aptitude the role requires (statistical analysis, guardrails, funnel measurement) is entirely absent

Current seniority level and scope (Senior/Staff on product features) falls well short of Principal IC6 expectations — no evidence of multi-workstream technical leadership or organization-wide systemic impact

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs. Principal IC6 scope
